$comment: Harness config read by .claude/hooks/*.mjs. stopGate = CHEAP checks (seconds each — the gate runs on EVERY turn end); keep the expensive full gate in /validate. Empty array or missing file disarms the gate. stopGateTimeoutSec caps each command, stopGateTotalSec caps the whole gate (must stay under the Stop hook's outer timeout in settings.json). Commands are repo-owner-trusted, run with a shell in the project root. workTracking is set by /harness-init; backend none disables the layer. baseBranch (null = main/master) names a non-standard integration branch (develop/trunk) — guard protects it and /review//validate diff against it. models maps ROLES (scout/build/deep) to model IDs per harness — the only file that names a model; /models re-verifies it and session-start warns when checkedAt is older than staleDays. review is NOT a role: the reviewer is the sibling of whoever implemented (deep<->build). The two halves are NOT symmetric. models.claude is PROSE-FACING (documentation): no code reads it, by design — Claude Code's family aliases (opus/sonnet/haiku) float to the newest family member on their own, so agent frontmatter carries the alias directly and it never churns. models.codex is LOAD-BEARING: Codex has no aliases, its IDs are pinned and churn, and cli/emit-codex.js bakes the resolved IDs into .codex/agents/*.toml at EMIT time — so changing them here does nothing until you re-emit (npx perfect-harness-engineering emit).
